title: gastrochilus penangianus
intro: Gastrochilus penangianus, also known as Penang orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Gastrochilus penangianus include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of Gastrochilus penangianus are typically made by infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
